install.title: Install Blok
install.description: Add Blok to your project using your favorite package manager.

configure.title: Import and configure
configure.description: Import the editor and tools, then configure your block types.

save.title: Save content
save.description: Extract clean JSON data ready to save anywhere.

quickStart.title: Quick Start
quickStart.description: Get up and running with Blok in just a few simple steps.

blokClass.title: Blok Class
blokClass.description: The main editor class that initializes and manages the Blok editor instance.

save.name: save()
save.description: Extracts the current editor content as structured JSON data. This is the primary method for persisting editor content.

render.name: render(data)
render.description: Renders editor content from previously saved JSON data.

focus.name: focus(atEnd?)
focus.description: Sets focus to the editor. Optionally positions cursor at the end of content.

clear.name: clear()
clear.description: Removes all blocks from the editor.

destroy.name: destroy()
destroy.description: Destroys the editor instance and removes all DOM elements and event listeners.

isReady.name: isReady
isReady.description: Promise that resolves when editor is ready

configuration.title: Configuration
configuration.description: The configuration object passed to the Blok constructor.

blocksApi.title: Blocks API
blocksApi.description: Manage blocks in the editor — create, delete, update, and reorder content.

blockApi.title: BlockAPI
blockApi.description: Interface for working with individual blocks. Returned by blocks.getById(), blocks.getBlockByIndex(), and blocks.insert().

caretApi.title: Caret API
caretApi.description: Control cursor position and selection within the editor.

eventsApi.title: Events API
eventsApi.description: Subscribe to and manage editor lifecycle events.

historyApi.title: History API
historyApi.description: Control undo/redo functionality for editor operations.

selectionApi.title: Selection API
selectionApi.description: Work with text selection within the editor.

stylesApi.title: Styles API
stylesApi.description: Access CSS class names for styling custom tools and UI elements.

toolbarApi.title: Toolbar API
toolbarApi.description: Control the block toolbar and its state.

inlineToolbarApi.title: InlineToolbar API
inlineToolbarApi.description: Control the inline formatting toolbar (bold, italic, etc.).

sanitizerApi.title: Sanitizer API
sanitizerApi.description: Clean and sanitize HTML content to prevent XSS attacks.

tooltipApi.title: Tooltip API
tooltipApi.description: Display tooltip hints on UI elements.

readOnlyApi.title: ReadOnly API
readOnlyApi.description: Control the read-only state of the editor.

i18nApi.title: I18n API
i18nApi.description: Internationalization support for translating UI strings.

uiApi.title: UI API
uiApi.description: Access to Blok UI elements and state.

listenersApi.title: Listeners API
listenersApi.description: Manage custom DOM event listeners with automatic cleanup.

outputData.title: OutputData
outputData.description: The data structure returned by the save() method.

blockData.title: BlockData
blockData.description: The structure of each block in the blocks array.

hero.title: Build beautiful
hero.description: A production-ready, extensible rich text editor that brings Notion-like block-based editing to your app. Customizable, themeable, and battle-tested.

features.description: Everything you need to ship a modern block editor: clean JSON, a full block library, and a deep, typed API.

cleanJson.title: Typed JSON, never HTML
cleanJson.description: Just typed JSON blocks, never an HTML blob.
cleanJson.learnMore: Learn more about Clean JSON output

details.summary: Blok is a headless editor built around a structured document model. Your app owns that data and decides how to store and display it.
details.benefit1: Typed JSON output, no HTML parsing
details.benefit2: Stable block IDs for diffing and version control
details.benefit3: Render on the server or the client
details.benefit4: Framework-agnostic core with full TypeScript types

blockLibrary.title: Every block, built in
blockLibrary.description: Notion-style blocks, ready in the box.
blockLibrary.learnMore: Learn more about the block library

details.summary: Blok includes the blocks most documents need, so you can put together rich content without building each one yourself.
details.benefit1: Tables with merged cells, resizing and cell colors
details.benefit4: Code, image, video, file and 100+ embeds

customBlocks.title: Bring your own blocks
customBlocks.description: Your own block tools, inline tools and block tunes.

details.summary: Built-in blocks cover most cases. When you need something specific, you can add your own without forking the editor.
details.benefit1: Custom block tools with a render/save API
details.benefit2: Custom inline tools and block tunes
details.benefit3: Lifecycle hooks: rendered, updated, moved, removed
details.benefit4: Public API for blocks, caret, history and selection

slashCommands.title: Type / to add anything
slashCommands.description: Type / to search and insert any block, or use Markdown shortcuts like #, -, 1., [] and >.
slashCommands.learnMore: Learn more about the slash menu and Markdown

details.summary: Type / to open a menu of everything you can add: headings, lists, images, tables and more. Pick one without reaching for the mouse.
details.benefit1: Start typing to filter the menu to what you want
details.benefit2: Markdown shortcuts: # for a heading, - for a list, > for a quote
details.benefit3: Or click the + button on any empty line

tables.title: Spreadsheet-grade tables
tables.description: Merged cells, column resizing, cell colors, heading rows and full keyboard navigation.

details.summary: The table block stores its layout as data, so merged cells and column widths survive a save and reload.

embeds.title: Embed 100+ services
embeds.description: Paste a link to embed 100+ services like YouTube, Figma, Spotify and Gist, or get an OpenGraph bookmark card.

details.summary: Paste a link and Blok works out how best to display it.
details.benefit2: Video, audio, social, code and design sources
details.benefit3: OpenGraph bookmark cards as a fallback
details.benefit4: Notion-style paste menu: link, bookmark or embed

undoRedo.title: Conflict-free undo & redo
undoRedo.description: Yjs-backed history with ⌘Z / ⌘⇧Z. Conflict-free, grouped at word boundaries, and it restores your caret.
undoRedo.learnMore: Learn more about undo and redo

details.summary: History is built on the same CRDT that powers live collaboration, so undo behaves predictably even when several people edit at once.

languages.title: 68 languages, RTL-ready
languages.description: Lazy-loaded translations for 68 locales with right-to-left support. Only your language ships to the browser.

details.summary: The editor's own interface is translated, so your users see Blok in their language.
details.benefit3: Lazy-loaded for a minimal base bundle

media.title: Image, video & audio
media.description: Drop in images, a YouTube-grade video player and audio with live waveforms. Every one a first-class block.

details.summary: Each kind of media gets its own block, with controls suited to how people use it.
details.benefit2: Video player with scrubber, speed, PiP & theater
details.benefit3: Audio blocks with cached waveform peaks
details.benefit4: Paste or drag a file to upload

migrationCard.title: Migrate from EditorJS
migrationCard.description: Already using EditorJS? Our automated codemod handles most of the transition — just run it, review the changes, and ship.

frameworks.description: A framework-agnostic core with a fully typed API, first-class React, Vue, and Angular adapters, and a zero-build CDN script. Pick whichever fits your project.
frameworks.vanillaDescription: Point Blok at any DOM node and go. Fully typed, no framework needed.
frameworks.reactDescription: The useBlok hook owns the editor lifecycle; <BlokContent> mounts it. React 18 and 19.
frameworks.vueDescription: Same shape as React: useBlok drives the editor, <BlokContent> mounts it. Vue 3.
frameworks.angularDescription: Add <blok-editor> and bind your tools. Standalone component, Angular 16+.
frameworks.cdnDescription: Add one script tag and use the BlokEditor global. Good for prototypes and no-build pages.

whyBlok.description: Most editors hand you a bare core and a long plugin shopping list. Blok comes with the batteries included: typed JSON output, a full block library, and a deep API, all working out of the box.
whyBlok.disclaimer: Comparison reflects each library's out-of-the-box defaults; all are extensible via plugins.

trusted.title: Companies that trust us
trusted.summary: Blok powers the internal knowledge base behind one of the world's largest pizza chains. Thousands of authors, one block model.
trusted.contactLead: We'll help you set it up and adapt it to exactly what you need.

footer.tagline: A friendly block-based rich text editor for modern applications.
footer.author: JackUait

search.noResultsDescription: Try different keywords or browse the documentation sections
search.emptyDescription: Find API methods, configuration options, tools, and more

demo.title: Try the
demo.subtitle: A fully interactive editor running right here in your browser. Type
demo.subtitleRest: for commands, drag blocks to reorder, or select text to format.
demo.hintInstantFeedbackDesc: Everything happens locally in your browser. No server roundtrips, just pure performance.
demo.hintCleanJsonDesc: Content is stored as structured JSON blocks. Parse, validate, and store anywhere.
demo.hintBlockArchDesc: Create custom block types with simple JavaScript classes. Fully extensible.
demo.outputInitialMessage: Click "Get JSON" to see the output

editorWrapper.buildHint: Make sure the Blok editor is built with

demo.welcomeParagraph: This is a live demo of the Blok editor. Try typing / to see available commands, or select text to format it.
demo.welcomeListItem3: Drag and drop to reorder blocks

migration.heroDescription: Migrate your project in minutes using our automated codemod.
migration.step1Description: One command handles imports, selectors, types, and configuration automatically.
migration.step2Description: The codemod handles all the breaking changes automatically.
migration.step3Description: Reference for manually updating your CSS selectors.
migration.step4Description: Custom block tools port over unchanged. Custom inline tools can be wrapped with wrapLegacyInlineTool for a fast path, or rewritten to the new MenuConfig render API for full feature access.
migration.aliasNoteDescription: Blok is exported as the default export and under an EditorJS alias, so you can keep your existing variable name and only change the import path. import Blok, import { Blok } and import { EditorJS } all resolve to the same class.
migration.aliasNoteCode: import { EditorJS } from '@jackuait/blok'; // new EditorJS({ ... }) stays unchanged
migration.customInlineToolAfter: render() { return { icon, name, onActivate, isActive }; }
migration.customInlineToolFastPathNote: Keep an existing EditorJS inline tool without a rewrite by wrapping its class. The shim routes onActivate → surround, isActive → checkState, forwards the static title/shortcut/sanitize and pulls the icon from the legacy render(). Without it, Blok silently skips a legacy HTMLElement render() and the tool vanishes. renderActions()/clear() are not bridged; the native MenuConfig rewrite remains recommended for full feature access.
migration.customBlockToolNote: render, save, validate, renderSettings, merge, onPaste and the toolbox/conversionConfig statics are read by the same keys. Optionally add setReadOnly(state) for cheaper read-only toggling.
migration.droppedFieldsWarning: When auto-migration drops a field with no Blok equivalent, Blok emits a console.warn prefixed [Blok migration] naming the block and field, so the loss is visible rather than silent. Capture any of these before migrating if you depend on them.
migration.ctaDescription: Start with the automated codemod and complete your migration in minutes.
migration.codemodApplyLabel: Apply the changes to your files
migration.codemodVerboseDescription: Show detailed output for each file processed
migration.supportedVersionsDescription: Coverage is per block/tool, not per Editor.js point release — what matters is which tools your content uses.
migration.supportedVersionsTarget: Blok targets the Editor.js 2.x line. The codemod is built against typical 2.x package versions (editorjs ^2.28, header ^2.8, paragraph ^2.11, list ^1.9) and the runtime reads the saved-data shapes those versions produce.
migration.supportedVersionsStubNote: Any block type Blok doesn't recognize and has no registered tool for renders as a stub and logs "Tool not found". Its data is preserved, so registering the tool later restores the block.

changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.description: Track every improvement, fix, and feature as Blok evolves
